items.title: Two Singaporean hikers killed after Indonesian volcano erupts
items.summary: NORTH HALMAHERA, Indonesia: Three hikers – two Singaporeans and a local – died in an eruption Friday of Indonesia's Mount Dukono volcano where they found themselves in a no-go zone, officials said. Twenty hikers were on the slopes when disaster struck, North Halmahera police chief Erlichson Pasaribu told reporters at a volcano monitoring station in Mamuya village. He said nine were from Singapore and the rest Indonesian.

items.title: Indonesia reports two suspected hantavirus cases
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia has reported two suspected hantavirus cases in Jakarta and Yogyakarta amid concerns over an outbreak linked to the cruise ship MV Hondius, the country's health ministry said on Friday, reported Xinhua. "Two suspected cases are currently undergoing confirmation," said Aji Muhawarman, head of the ministry's communication and public information bureau. Indonesia recorded 23 confirmed cases between 2024 and 2026, with three deaths, he said.

items.title: Two killed as car hits utility pole in Maran
items.summary: MARAN: Two men were killed when their car veered off the road and crashed into a utility pole at Jalan Besar Felda Jengka 12 here last night. District police chief Superintendent Wong Kim Wai said the accident occurred about 10pm while the victims were travelling from Jengka 13 towards Bandar Pusat Jengka. The deceased have been identified as the driver, Mohamad Arif Danial Ramli, 23, from Felda Jengka 22, and his passenger, Mohamad Ali Mohamed Saat, 31, from Felda Jengka 11.

items.title: Landslide hits Kampung Bukit Sungai Putih in Hulu Langat, residents told to evacuate home for safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — Residents of a house were instructed to evacuate their home after a landslide hit Kampung Bukit Sungai Putih, Hulu Langat near here yesterday afternoon. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they received a call at 2.17 pm and dispatched a six-man team from the Ampang Fire and Rescue Station with one engine to the location of the landslide, which arrived five minutes later. “A landslide about 1.2 metres was found and the residents of a house were instructed to vacate their home as a precautionary measure,” he said in a statement last night.

items.title: #NSTviral: Illegal dumpers caught on CCTV in Shah Alam [WATCH]
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Two men were recently caught on CCTV illegally dumping rubbish in Shah Alam and were ordered by the authorities to clean up the mess. The one-minute video was shared on Facebook by Majlis Bandaraya Shah Alam (MBSA). MBSA is actively monitoring illegal dumping hotspots around Shah Alam.

items.title: Navigating a world on fire
items.summary: SIXTY-NINE days into a war that nobody in Southeast Asia provoked, let alone started, Asean leaders gathered in Cebu, confronting an uncomfortable reality: even regions that pride themselves on strategic non-alignment are not shielded from the consequences of great-power conflict. Energy prices remain elevated, shipping insurance premiums have shot through the roof, and food inflation, already politically sensitive across much of the region, has worsened. That was the backdrop to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's participation in the 48th Asean Summit.

items.title: Hamzah ‘Reset’ gathering triggers Bersatu purge as MPs and assemblymen face suspension, expulsion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — The Bersatu Disciplinary Board has expelled and suspended the membership of several elected representatives and party leaders, effective immediately. They are: Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz (Tanah Merah); Datuk Abdul Khalib Abdullah (Rompin); Nordin Ahmad Ismail (Lumut); Datuk Rosol Wahid (Hulu Terengganu); Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor (Ketereh) Also suspended are Ahmad Man (Adun for Kuala Sepetang) and Syed Lukman Hakim Syed Mohd Zain (Adun for Chenderoh). When contacted by Bernama, Nordin Ahmad Ismail confirmed receiving the suspension letter.

items.title: Kedah hit by flash floods after two-hour downpour, 17 homes affected in Bandar Baharu
items.summary: “Twelve homes are in Kampung Sungai Tengas, two in Kampung Air Itam Dalam and one each in Kampung Cherok Meranti, Kampung Paya Salak and Kampung Batu Hampar,” he said in a statement last night, adding that seven families are being housed at the relief centre at Dewan KEDA Sungai Tengas. Meanwhile, he said that the water level at Sungai Tengas was rising while Sungai Air Itam Dalam and Kampung Cherok Meranti were dropping. The water levels of Sungai Terunsing and Sungai Terap Dalam were at danger levels, while the water level of Sungai Punti was still normal, he added.

items.title: PHRS leaders lodge police reports over defamatory Facebook posts
items.summary: TAWAU: Two police reports have been lodged against a Facebook account owner for allegedly uploading defamatory posts linking recent police arrests to the leadership of Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah (PHRS). Norazian said she discovered the posts while browsing Facebook the previous night. She noted that the content featured photographs of party leaders alongside images of police arrests and court proceedings.

items.title: WHO chief due in Canaries to coordinate hantavirus ship evacuation
items.summary: GENEVA: The World Health Organization's chief is due in the Spanish island of Tenerife on Saturday to help coordinate the evacuation of passengers hit by the hantavirus, Spanish ministry sources said. The only hantavirus strain that can transmit from person to person – Andes virus – has been confirmed among those who have tested positive, fuelling international concern. "The virus is not that contagious that it easily jumps from person to person," he said.

items.title: Housing ministry studying 'Purchase Option' mechanism to curb abandoned housing projects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Housing and Local Government Ministry is studying the introduction of an "Option to Purchase" clause under the proposed Housing Development Bill as part of efforts to strengthen the country's property sector and curb delayed, sick and abandoned housing projects. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the proposed policy reform would provide greater flexibility for both buyers and developers to withdraw before signing the Sale and Purchase Agreement (SPA), thereby reducing unnecessary financial and legal commitments at an early stage. He said the proposed purchase option mechanism would also enable developers to better gauge actual market demand and project viability before construction begins.

items.title: US imposes sanctions on companies it accuses of aiding Iran's weapons sector
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US Treasury on Friday announced sanctions against 10 individuals and companies, including several in China and Hong Kong, over accusations they aided Iran's efforts to secure weapons and the raw materials needed to build its Shahed drones and ballistic missiles. In a statement, Treasury said it remained ready to take economic action against Iran's military industrial base to prevent Teheran from reconstituting its production capacity. Treasury said it was also prepared to act against any foreign company supporting illicit Iranian commerce, including airlines, and could impose secondary sanctions on foreign financial institutions that aid Iran's efforts, including those connected to China's independent "teapot" oil refineries.

items.title: Malaysia urged to cement leadership as global sukuk tops US$1tri [WATCH]
items.summary: Islamic financial products, once viewed as a niche segment of global finance, have now firmly established themselves within the mainstream international financial system, said Ji Zhuang, Bloomberg head of indices for Asia Pacific. He said the rapid expansion of Islamic finance will stall without the market standardisation required for reliable scalability. Ji said Malaysia must continue driving innovation while developing local talent for the global sukuk market to move beyond the US$1 trillion mark, as estimated by Bloomberg.

items.title: Ascend Airways Malaysia to wet lease three Boeing B737-800NG to AirBorneo?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ascend Airways Malaysia (AAM) is deploying three Boeing B737-800NG (next generation) aircraft for passenger operations as its UK affiliate winds down operations in a broader restructuring within parent Avia Solutions Group (ASG). The Malaysian-registered aircraft, crew, maintenance and insurance (ACMI) provider already received one B737-800NG passenger aircraft recently and expects to receive another two this month. "Following the recent addition of its first B737-800NG passenger aircraft, AAM expects to receive an additional two B737-800 passenger aircraft in May 2026," AAM told Business Times recently.

items.title: US plans evacuation flight for Americans on hantavirus ship
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States said Friday it was organising an evacuation flight for Americans on a hantavirus-struck cruise ship that has sailed to the Canary Islands. "The Department of State is arranging a repatriation flight to support the safe return of American passengers on this ship," a State Department spokesperson said. The ship operator earlier said that 17 Americans were on board.

items.title: Boeing probing worker death as union calls for accountability
items.summary: BENGALURU: Boeing said on Friday it was continuing to investigate a workplace accident that led to the death of an employee last month, after a union called for full accountability over the incident. "We continue to investigate last month's workplace accident...", a Boeing spokesperson said. The company said it continues to prioritise worker safety, including holding "dedicated safety stand downs" to review processes and improve workplace conditions.

items.title: US fire on Iran tankers sparks reprisals as deal hangs in balance
items.summary: TEHERAN: A US fighter jet disabled two Iranian-flagged tankers to enforce a port blockade on Friday, prompting retaliatory attacks and rattling a shaky truce as Teheran weighed Washington's latest proposal to end the Middle East war. Iranian officials accused the United States of violating the ceasefire with the tanker strikes and hampering diplomatic efforts to end the conflict. Iran-backed Hizbollah launched missiles and drones at military bases in Israel in retaliation for a recent attack on Beirut and ongoing strikes in the south, where Lebanese authorities reported 11 people killed on Friday.

items.title: WHO reports six confirmed hantavirus cases tied to Spain-bound cruise
items.summary: BENGALURU: The World Health Organization said on Friday that eight people had fallen ill, including three who died, in a hantavirus outbreak linked to a cruise ship, with six confirmed cases and two probable cases. The ship had 147 passengers and crew on board when the outbreak was first reported on May 2, while 34 others had already left the vessel. The CDC said it plans to evacuate American passengers aboard the ship on a US government medical repatriation flight to Omaha, Nebraska.

items.title: IMF board clears Pakistan to access US$1.32 billion
items.summary: The IMF said Pakistan would be able to draw around US$1.1 billion under the Extended Fund Facility and about US$220 million under the Resilience and Sustainability Facility, lifting total disbursements under the two ongoing programmes to about US$4.8 billion. Earlier on Friday, Reuters reported, citing sources, that the IMF's executive board voted to approve an agreement it previously reached with Pakistan. The country is on a US$7 billion IMF programme and reached a staff-level agreement in March.

items.title: MMEA to boost Selangor maritime surveillance with radar expansion and drones
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) will strengthen surveillance capabilities in waters off Selangor through expanded radar coverage and the use of drones to combat maritime crime effectively. He said the enhancement formed part of a broader strategic approach to improve maritime enforcement capabilities nationwide. Muhaimin said the strategic improvements were necessary as maritime enforcement agencies continued facing evolving operational challenges, particularly along Selangor's coastline.

items.title: Crypto robbery case: Probe taking too long, says lawyer
items.summary: The lawyer, identified only as Charles, said he would take the case to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) if there was no word from the police. Charles said technical and digital forensic reports in such cases would typically take between one and two months to complete. Earlier yesterday, Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said investigations into the case were pending technical reports.

items.title: One year on, how Warisan KL has breathed new life back to Kuala Lumpur’s past — and what’s coming up next
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — When he launched Warisan KL in April 2025, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was aiming to revitalise the heart and soul of Kuala Lumpur. They are: Carcosa and Seri Negara The Kuala Lumpur Old Railway Station Dataran Merdeka heritage area, including Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad The Merdeka 118 precinct Petaling Street Masjid Jamek enclave Central Market Medan Pasar Dayabumi Complex & City Point Dayabumi Masjid Negara One year on, Malay Mail takes a look at how much has been done and what is next. — Picture by Choo Choy May Under Khazanah Nasional’s stewardship, Seri Negara and Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad reopened to the public recently after a 10-month round-the-clock restoration exercise.

items.title: Syria says arrested Assad-era general over chemical attack
items.summary: DAMASCUS: Syria's interior ministry on Friday announced the arrest of a general from ousted president Bashar al-Assad's era, accusing him of involvement in a 2013 chemical attack on a suburb of the capital, Damascus. In August 2013, the army under Assad's rule was accused of using chemical weapons to target areas then under rebel control, killing more than 1,400 men, women and children, according to US intelligence and rights groups. With Syria at the height of its civil war, the Assad government denied responsibility, but agreed to hand over its chemical arsenal in order to avert US strikes.

items.title: Golden Trump statue is no idol for worship, pastor insists
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A massive gilded statue of President Donald Trump installed at his Miami golf course is definitely not a "golden calf" to be worshipped, the pastor who led a dedication ceremony for the eyebrow-raising effigy insisted Friday. But the "Don Colossus" statue, which depicts Trump raising his fist after surviving an assassination attempt in 2024, was finally unveiled Wednesday at Trump National Doral. "This statue is not about worship.

items.title: Russia and Ukraine agree on three-day US-mediated ceasefire
items.summary: MOSCOW/WASHINGTON: Russia and Ukraine confirmed on Friday that they had agreed to a three-day ceasefire announced by US President Donald Trump that will run from May 9 to May 11. Trump's announcement on Truth Social also said each country, locked in more than four years of conflict, would be exchanging 1,000 prisoners of war. Both Kyiv and Moscow have accused the other of violating ceasefires declared separately this week as Russia readies to hold a Victory Day parade on May 9 that marks the 1945 Soviet victory over Nazi Germany.

items.title: From jewellery to private letters, Marilyn Monroe's hidden world heads to auction
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: Memorabilia belonging to late model and movie star Marilyn Monroe is heading to the auction block, offering a rare glimpse into the private world of one of Hollywood's most enduring legends. Items up for sale include pieces from her wardrobe, jewellery, letters, handwritten notes, paintings and poetry. Born Norma Jeane Mortenson in Los Angeles in 1926, Monroe became a renowned actress, model, singer and sex symbol known for her blonde hair and hourglass body.

items.title: Oil slick off Iran's Kharg Island
items.summary: WASHINGTON: An oil slick is spreading off the coast of Iran's Kharg Island, a key oil export terminal for the Islamic republic, The New York Times reported Friday, citing satellite images. Kharg Island is at the heart of Iran's oil export industry, a lynchpin of the country's battered economy, and lies in the Gulf, north of the narrow, strategic Strait of Hormuz. Kharg Island has Iran's largest oil terminal, oil pipelines, storage tanks and related infrastructure.

items.title: US says opened fire to disable two Iran-flagged ships violating port blockade
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US forces fired on and disabled two Iranian-flagged tankers that attempted to violate the American blockade of Iran's ports on Friday, the US military said. Washington has now forcibly halted four ships it said were attempting to violate the blockade, which has been in place since April 13. And on April 19, the Iranian-flagged M/V Touska attempted to violate the blockade and ignored multiple warnings from a US destroyer, CENTCOM said at the time.

items.title: GOF seizes silica sand, processing equipment worth RM7.25mil in Kampar
items.summary: IPOH: About 5,000 tonnes of silica sand and mineral processing equipment worth RM7.25 million were seized on Thursday in Kampar during an integrated operation at a premises found to be operating without a valid permit. General Operations Force (GOF) Northern Brigade commander SAC Balveer Singh Mahindar Singh said that three local men aged between 20 and 30, believed to be business owners and lorry drivers, were arrested during the operation codenamed Op Bersepadu Khazanah. Balveer Singh said the operation was carried out following intelligence gathered on a premises in Kampar suspected of conducting silica sand processing activities without a valid permit.

items.title: Boy killed after being crushed by concrete block in Pasir Puteh
items.summary: Pasir Puteh district police chief Superintendent Saari Yaacob said family members later informed authorities that both boys had fallen into a sand pit before they were found trapped under concrete blocks following sand erosion. The injured victim was taken to Tengku Anis Hospital for further treatment. Meanwhile, Jejak Jalanan activist Tengku Mohd Harith Hakimin Tengku Abdul Azis said the victim and his family were recipients of monthly aid from the non-governmental organisation (NGO).

items.title: Hiker dies at summit of Bukit Gunong in Bachok
items.summary: Bachok Fire and Rescue Station chief Ahmad Ridhuan Mohamad Ghazali said the operation to bring down the victim was led by operation commander Fire Officer I Ibrahim Mohd Ghani and six personnel. "It took personnel 20 minutes to bring the victim down the hill. Meanwhile, Bachok district police chief Superintendent Mohamad Ismail Jamaluddin said the victim's death was believed to have been caused by a heart attack.

items.title: PM backs Sultan of Selangor's concerns, cites 'too many excesses' in pig farming sector
items.summary: CEBU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the concerns raised by Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah over pig farming in the state were justified due to repeated excesses that have affected public health. Anwar said that, in principle, he did not agree with pig farms operating without strict regulation, particularly when located too close to residential areas. There must be strict controls, and the farms must be located far away from residential areas.

items.title: Firefighters rescue 11 hikers trapped in tunnel due to strong currents
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mokhtar said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 4.11pm today. He said six personnel from the Selayang Fire and Rescue Station, together with a fire engine, were dispatched to the location, which is about 21km from the station. "Personnel also used rescue ropes and personal flotation device (PFD) equipment during the rescue operation.

items.title: Asean leaders pledge security and stability in regional waters in line with UNCLOS
items.summary: In an Asean Leaders' Declaration on Maritime Cooperation adopted at the just-concluded 48th Asean Summit in Cebu, the Philippines, the leaders stressed that UNCLOS remains the legal framework governing all activities in the oceans and seas. The declaration highlighted the need to strengthen maritime cooperation in areas such as maritime law enforcement, maritime transport connectivity, search and rescue operations, marine environmental protection, blue economy initiatives, ocean governance, marine scientific research, technology transfer, information sharing, and maritime domain awareness. Asean leaders also agreed to advance the work of the Asean Coast Guard Forum as a platform for cooperation among maritime law enforcement agencies to address maritime security challenges, transnational crime, and other cross-border threats.

items.title: Six senior civil service appointments unveiled, government says move boosts efficiency and integrity in ministries
items.summary: Under the reshuffle, Ministry of Education (MOE) secretary-general Datuk Dr Aminuddin Hassim has been appointed secretary-general of the Ministry of Higher Education. Aminuddin’s post will be assumed by MOE deputy secretary-general (Planning and Development) Datuk Wan Hashim Wan Rahim. Also involved in the reshuffle are Datuk Dr Megat Sany Megat Ahmad Supian, who was appointed deputy secretary-general (Planning and Development) of MOE, and Datuk Bahria Mohd Tamil, who was appointed deputy secretary-general (Strategic Communications and Creative Industry) at the Ministry of Communications.

items.title: Honda City chase through Federal Highway ends in crash near Damansara Heights, police arrest two foreigners
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Police arrested two foreigners after a high-speed chase ended with their car crashing along Jalan Johor near Pavillion Damansara Heights early this morning. Petaling Jaya district police chief said that the police tried to stop a Honda City that was heading to the capital along the Federal Highway for inspection at 2.15am but the car sped away, leading to a high-speed chase along the SPRINT Highway, where the car skidded and crashed into a signboard at the U-turn as it was headed to Kuala Lumpur. “The police arrested the male driver and a woman passenger, both foreigners in their 20s at the crash, while another suspect managed to run away.

items.title: Crocodile attack suspected in Sarawak after missing fisherman’s remains discovered during Sungai Ridan search
items.summary: MIRI, May 8 — Body parts of an elderly man reported missing while fishing in Sungai Ridan, Marudi, yesterday have been found. “At 10.52am today, the search and rescue team discovered parts of the victim’s body, namely two legs, a head and a hand. “At 4pm, the search operation was fully concluded as the remaining body parts could not be found.

items.title: Deputy minister Sim stresses not all EVs will become expensive as CKD models remain accessible
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, May 8 — Claims that all electric vehicles (EVs) will become more expensive following the reimposition of import duties on completely built-up (CBU) EVs from July are inaccurate, said Deputy Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti) Minister Sim Tze Tzin. He said consumers will still have access to a wide range of reasonably priced EVs through completely knocked-down (CKD) models. Sim said the CKD strategy would ensure EVs remain affordable within the RM100,000 to RM200,000 price range, thereby maintaining public access to EV technology.

items.title: PM Anwar reassures nation: Fuel supply steadier after enforcement and import push
items.summary: CEBU, May 8 — The government has assured that the nation’s petrol and diesel supply is more stable following the government’s proactive measures, particularly under the management of Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said reducing RON95 consumption, tightening enforcement to curb diesel leakages and smuggling, as well as importing fuel supplies, have helped strengthen the country’s supply security. He said Asean countries also discussed joint efforts to address supply shortages and ease rising cost pressures, including on food, fertiliser and transportation.

items.title: KTMB rolls out 186 extra trains from May 22 to June 7 for Hari Raya Aidiladha and school holidays
items.summary: KTMB said that the additional trains will create 58,380 seats for the increased passenger demand, with tickets being sold from noon tomorrow (May 9) via the KITS Style app, KTMB official website and KTMB kiosk tickets at selected stations. The KL Sentral-Padang Besar-KL Sentral route will offer 15,702 tickets for journeys from May 22 to 26, May 29 to 31 and June 4 to7, while there will be 7,584 tickets for the KL Sentral-Butterworth-KL Sentral and KL Sentral-Ipoh-KL Sentral routes respectively for the same period. Also, 18,960 tickets for the KL Sentral-JB Sentral-KL Sentral route are available for journeys from May 22 to 26, May 29 to 31 and June 1 to 7, while 9,180 tickets are available for the Klang-Ipoh-Klang EMU Plus route for journeys from May 22 to 24, May 29 to 31 and June 5 to 7.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan police record 49 statements in probe over social media posts on royalty and state issues
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 8 — Negeri Sembilan police have recorded statements from 49 individuals to assist investigations into statements and the dissemination of social media content related to current issues involving the state’s royalty and administration. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said investigations were launched following several police reports lodged over content deemed provocative and capable of creating anxiety and confusion among the public. He also advised the public not to issue statements or spread content touching on 3R issues (religion, race and royalty), especially through social media, that are provocative and could threaten public order.

items.title: Housing ministry eyes option to purchase clause from new bill to reduce risks of sick and abandoned projects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — The Housing and Local Government Ministry (KPKT) is studying to introduce an Option to Purchase (OTP) clause under the proposed Real Property Development Bill, aimed at giving homebuyers and developers greater flexibility to withdraw before signing the Sales and Purchase Agreement (SPA). Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the proposed measure would help both parties reduce unnecessary financial and legal commitments at an early stage. Nga said the initiative forms part of the broader Reformasi Perumahan Madani agenda aimed at modernising Malaysia’s housing regulatory framework and ensuring long-term industry sustainability.

items.title: Cloud seeding set for Perlis and Kedah from May 10 as dams hit alert levels, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Cloud Seeding Operations (OPA) Series 5 and 6 will be conducted in Perlis and Kedah from May 10 to 12 and May 17 to 19 respectively, said Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup. He said the operations were carried out as a proactive measure to increase rainfall distribution in the main dam catchment areas. Arthur said the OPA would be implemented based on meteorological analyses conducted by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), taking into account factors such as air humidity, atmospheric stability and wind movement patterns to ensure the effectiveness of the operation.

items.title: Melaka magistrates hear guilty pleas from teenage pair accused of hiding baby’s birth and preventing live delivery
items.summary: MELAKA, May 8 — A young couple pleaded guilty at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court today to two separate charges involving the concealment of a baby’s birth and the consumption of medication to prevent a live birth last month. Adam Harith Ahmad, 18, and his girlfriend, aged 17 years and 10 months, entered their pleas after the charges were read before Magistrates Sharda Shienha Mohd Suleiman and Uthman Abd Ghani. Magistrate Sharda Shienha subsequently allowed the accused bail at RM3,500 and set June 10 for the mention of the case while awaiting chemistry reports and a social report from the Social Welfare Department (JKM).

items.title: Malaysia is expensive? Local tourism group say perception matters more than numbers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Claims on Indonesian social media suggesting Malaysia has become too expensive for tourists have raised concern among tourism industry players, though arrivals have not been significantly affected. Malaysian Association of Tour and Travel Agents president Datuk Mohd Khalid Harun said the narrative could still influence how potential visitors perceive the country, Harian Metro reported today. He said Malaysia remains a strong destination for Indonesian travellers due to cultural proximity, language familiarity, halal food options, frequent flights and family ties.

items.title: Queen praises Uzbekistan’s beauty, Islamic heritage and hospitality
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia has expressed admiration for Uzbekistan’s architectural beauty, Islamic heritage and the warm hospitality of its people during her recent visit to the Central Asian nation. In a post titled “Rahmat, Uzbekistan!” dated May 7 and shared on the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar today, Raja Zarith Sofiah described the visit as a meaningful experience filled with fond memories. “For a long time, many people, including myself, only thought of travelling to Western countries.

items.title: MBPJ activates 24-hour flood hotline as heavy rain triggers flash floods in Petaling Jaya
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 8 — The Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) activated its disaster operations room and emergency hotline following continuous heavy rain and storm conditions today. In a Facebook statement, MBPJ urged the public to report incidents such as fallen trees, flash floods and other weather-related emergencies through its 24-hour hotline at 03-7957 4113/03-7954 2020. The adverse weather has already led to flash floods, fallen trees and traffic congestion in parts of Petaling Jaya and other parts of Selangor and Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Baru Bian moves to strike out PKR’s RM10m lawsuit over party exit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Ba’kelalan Assemblyman Baru Bian has filed an application to strike out a lawsuit brought against him by Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR), which is seeking RM10 million in damages over an alleged breach of bond conditions after he left the party following the 14th General Election (GE14) in 2018. Baru Bian is among 16 former MPs and assemblymen being sued by PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh on behalf of the party for allegedly breaching the terms of a RM10 million bond signed with PKR. Others named as defendants are Datuk Kamarudin Jaffar, Datuk Mansor Othman, Datuk Mohd Rashid Hasnon, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah and Datuk Seri R. Santhara Kumar.

items.title: Bank Negara’s international reserves at RM508.4b as of April 30
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Bank Negara Malaysia’s (BNM) international reserves stood at US$129.7 billion (about RM508.4 billion) as at April 30, 2026. BNM said the gross international reserves comprise foreign currency reserves (US$113.8 billion), International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserves position (US$1.3 billion), special drawing rights (US$5.9 billion), gold (US$6.4 billion) and other reserve assets (US$2.3 billion). Meanwhile, the central bank said its total assets amounted to RM615.55 billion, comprising mainly gold, foreign financial assets and other reserves, including special drawing rights (RM523.69 billion), Malaysian government papers (RM13.10 billion), loans and advances (RM28.92 billion), deposits with financial institutions (RM340.61 million), property and equipment (RM4.55 billion) and other assets (RM44.95 billion).

items.title: Will political funding Bill be ready before GE16? Law minister Azalina says ‘I honestly do not know’
items.summary: MELAKA, May 8 — The proposed Political Funding Bill is still under study and has yet to secure consensus from all political parties, Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said today. The de facto law minister said agreement from all parties is essential as they will be the main stakeholders and users of the proposed legislation. She said discussions on political funding are ongoing, with several key policy issues still unresolved, including public funding for parties, contribution limits and the body that will oversee political funds.

items.title: Road users advised to plan journeys ahead of KL run around Padang Merbok tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — The public is advised to plan their journeys to avoid traffic congestion during the “XTEP 10KM Time Trial 2026” running programme in the capital tomorrow. Head of the Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT), ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa, said the programme would take place from 5am to 9am around Padang Merbok. Mohd Zamzuri said the route would begin at Padang Merbok and pass through Jalan Berjasa, Jalan Sultan Salahuddin, Bulatan Dato Onn, Jalan Raja Laut, Jalan Putra, Jalan Tun Ismail, Jalan Dato Onn, Lebuhraya Sultan Iskandar (Mahameru) and Jalan Parlimen.

items.title: Ex-Armed Forces chief’s RM3m graft case: 23 documents submitted to defence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — The defence in former chief of Defence Force Tan Sri Mohd Nizam Jaffar’s case has received 23 documents, including recorded statements, in relation to four charges involving abuse of power and criminal breach of trust amounting to RM3 million. Lawyer Abdul Aziz Hamzah, representing Mohd Nizam, 59, said the additional documents were handed over by the prosecution during case mention proceedings before Sessions Court judge Rosli Ahmad today. “Today, the prosecution submitted documents related to the charges as requested by the defence under Section 51A of the Criminal Procedure Code.

items.title: Storm hits Klang Valley: Flash floods, toppled trees and road congestion from Ampang to Meru and beyond (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Flash floods hit several parts of the Klang Valley this afternoon following hours of heavy rain, causing traffic congestion and disruption in Kuala Lumpur and Selangor. In the Klang Valley, heavy rain led to rising water levels in several areas, with motorists reporting slow-moving traffic and flooded roads in multiple locations on social media platforms. In an updated alert, MetMalaysia said the same stormy conditions are expected to continue till 6pm in Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, Negeri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor.

items.title: Five-month-old boy killed after drug-positive lorry driver crashes into Perodua Myvi on Baling road
items.summary: BALING, May 8 — A five-month-old baby boy in Kedah was killed while his parents were injured in a collision with a lorry driven by a man who tested positive for drugs. Baling district police chief Superintendent Brandon Richard Joe said the infant was pronounced dead at Baling Hospital’s emergency ward. Police arrested the 43-year-old lorry driver after his initial urine screening returned positive for drugs.

items.title: Perak fireman in the dock with seven counts of sexual assault against 14-year-old girl in Parit
items.summary: IPOH, May 8 — A 28-year-old fireman was charged at the Sessions Court here today with seven counts of sexual offences involving a 14-year-old boarding school student in Parit, Perak. Muhammad Aidilafifi Muhammad claimed trial before judge Jean Sharmila Jesudason, several local news outlets including The Star and the New Straits Times reported. Muhammad Aidilafifi is also facing a separate charge for possessing an obscene image of the teenage girl.

items.title: Let court decide on compensation for Jalan Masjid India sinkhole victim, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 8 — Claims for compensation involving an Indian woman tourist who died after falling into a sinkhole at Jalan Masjid India, Kuala Lumpur, will be left to the court to decide, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh. She said the victim’s family could submit their claim through the courts as Kuala Lumpur had never faced such a case before. The media previously reported that the family of the woman tourist, G Vijaya Lakshmi, 48, was seeking appropriate compensation and planning to meet Hannah and Fadlun within two weeks to one month after Vijaya’s death certificate was issued yesterday.

items.title: Grab rolls out Group Ride feature nationwide as govt pushes smarter commuting
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA May 8 — Grab Malaysia today announced its new Group Ride feature that allows multiple passengers travelling along similar routes to share a single e-hailing ride and split fares. The feature enables users to arrange shared rides with friends, colleagues or family members through flexible pick-up and drop-off options. The Group Ride feature is being rolled out progressively to eligible Grab users nationwide through the mobile application.

items.title: Police: Man in his 80s killed in violent robbery at Ayer Tawar home, wife hospitalised
items.summary: Manjung police chief ACP Hasbullah Abd Rahman said police received a call from a member of the public at about 5.30am informing them of the robbery. Neighbours who heard the commotion came to assist the victims and took them to Seri Manjung Hospital for treatment,” he said in a statement today. He said police intelligence and investigations led to the arrest of a 27-year-old male suspect, who has seven previous criminal records, at about 7.45pm yesterday.

items.title: Court extends remand of two suspects in Ketereh college student murder by a week
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 8 — Two youths detained to assist in the investigation into the murder of a female college student, who was found with 61 stab wounds in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, on May 1, have had their remand extended for another seven days. The remand order against the two 19-year-old suspects was issued by Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid at the Magistrate’s Court here today. The two suspects had previously been remanded for seven days, which ended today, to assist investigations under Section 302.

items.title: Sabah manufacturers say ports in ‘paralysis’, could drive up prices and cost jobs if prolonged
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 8 — The Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing (FMM) Sabah Branch has slammed the worsening congestion at Sabah ports, describing the prolonged disruption as a man-made logistical crisis that is crippling the state’s industrial competitiveness and pushing businesses towards collapse. FMM Sabah chairman Liaw Hen Kong said port operations in the state had reached a “state of paralysis”, with severe disruptions to container trucking, frequent vessel omissions, massive container rollovers and prolonged delays in gate-in and gate-out activities becoming the “new normal”. “Due to port incompetence, businesses pay the price,” Liaw stressed.
